signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBA Pivot
signals.text: GBP/AUD G8 spread at 1.309% confirms a dominant bilateral theme as GBP outperforms (+0.552%) while AUD lags (-0.757%). AUD is under pressure following weak domestic labor data and headlines suggesting the RBA hike cycle is nearing its end. Despite the RBA 4.35% vs BoE 3.75% carry advantage for AUD, the 60bp premium is being offset by growth concerns. GBP/AUD at 1.8805.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Options
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.04 remains supported by a +277bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage. While claims of a US-Iran draft agreement provide a temporary risk-on tailwind for the Nikkei (+2.29%), Middle East uncertainty continues to support USD on dips. Options market shows a moderate put skew (25d RR -1.32), signaling institutional hedging against BoJ intervention as price holds near the 160.00 threshold. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 16.8 (normal volatility).

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (long% 22.3%) shows speculators heavily fading the currency despite a hawkish market bias for the RBNZ. With the RBNZ meeting on 27 May and a market bias for a hike, the crowded spec short position faces significant unwind risk if the central bank delivers. NZD/USD at 0.5873; a hawkish hold or hike targets a squeeze toward 0.6000.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7868 exhibits an extreme Vol Risk Premium (VRP) of +6.1%, with IV at 13.0% vs HV30 of 6.9%. This suggests the options market is pricing in significantly higher volatility than recently realized, likely due to safe-haven CHF demand competing with the massive +375bp USD carry advantage. The bear flattener regime in US yields (2s10s +54.6bp) supports the USD leg, but the vol premium indicates high protection costs for further CHF weakness.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Volatility
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1614 remains in a structural downtrend supported by a +157bp SOFR-€STR carry advantage for the USD. COT data shows specs are net short -8,151, aligning with the 1M trend (-1.08%). Elevated hedging demand is evident with a +2.2% VRP (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.9%), while the 25d RR at -0.47 shows a mild put skew. Market is paying a premium for downside protection rather than chasing the move.
